full_text:
Meeting to combine LGSC in FINPROVE and IMPRESS

Meeting to combine LGSC in FINPROVE and IMPRESS

Date: 24.4.2026

Date: 24.4.2026

Participants: Anniina Färkkilä, Katriina Jalkanen, Tanja Juslin, Anni Suoknuuti, Line Bjørge

Participants: Anniina Färkkilä, Katriina Jalkanen, Tanja Juslin, Anni Suoknuuti, Line Bjørge

We will aim to join the LGSC cohorts from FINPROVE and IMPRESS.

We will aim to join the LGSC cohorts from FINPROVE and IMPRESS.

Focus is on LGSC both with and without MEK inhibitor treatments

Focus is on LGSC both with and without MEK inhibitor treatments

Scientific questions:

Scientific questions:

What transcriptomic & spatial proteomic factors drive heterogeneous responses to MEK inhibitors in LGSC?

What transcriptomic & spatial proteomic factors drive heterogeneous responses to MEK inhibitors in LGSC?

Explain why patients with similar MAPK pathway mutations show variable clinical outcomes

Explain why patients with similar MAPK pathway mutations show variable clinical outcomes

Investigate mechanisms of response in patients lacking targetable MAPK pathway alterations

Investigate mechanisms of response in patients lacking targetable MAPK pathway alterations

Investigate how targetable mutations affect the tumor biology in LGSC

Investigate how targetable mutations affect the tumor biology in LGSC

Numbers so far LSGC

Numbers so far LSGC

profiled: FINPROVE n=XX, IMPRESS =

profiled: FINPROVE n=XX, IMPRESS =

MEK treated:

MEK treated:

To combine datasets, The Helsinki team will merge perform NanoString gene expression and CyCIF analyses using a unified pipeline.

To combine datasets, The Helsinki team will merge perform NanoString gene expression and CyCIF analyses using a unified pipeline.

Genomics has been done with different platforms. This will not be the primary focus initially (to revisit later).

Genomics has been done with different platforms. This will not be the primary focus initially (to revisit later).

Both teams need to align definitions of good vs poor response across cohorts before analyses

Both teams need to align definitions of good vs poor response across cohorts before analyses

Additional real-world patients as “Control” arm: Both teams will gather also patients from real-world data from historical cohorts in Bergen and Helsinki (Oncosys-OVA). Anni will send the clinical data requirements to Line for the ethical approval application.

Additional real-world patients as “Control” arm: Both teams will gather also patients from real-world data from historical cohorts in Bergen and Helsinki (Oncosys-OVA). Anni will send the clinical data requirements to Line for the ethical approval application.

We need to decide on Genomic profiling need and MEK inhibitor usage

We need to decide on Genomic profiling need and MEK inhibitor usage

MTA drafting. Katriina will consult the HUS lawyer whether the agreement should be IMPRESS–HUS or IMPRESS–University of Helsinki (Färkkilä Lab).

MTA drafting. Katriina will consult the HUS lawyer whether the agreement should be IMPRESS–HUS or IMPRESS–University of Helsinki (Färkkilä Lab).

After that Line and Anniina will initiate the process.

After that Line and Anniina will initiate the process.

Funding: Anni will apply for the NSGO grant in September. Katriina: FINPROVE Syöpäsäätiö funding can also be used for profiling of the Norwegian samples.

Funding: Anni will apply for the NSGO grant in September. Katriina: FINPROVE Syöpäsäätiö funding can also be used for profiling of the Norwegian samples.

Publication: We are flexible with authorships. PhD thesis position for Anni, the leaders are Anniina and Heini. From Bergen Line and Cecilie (postdoc). Please note that the study authorship leads will need to be included (Åslaug Helland, Katriina Jalkanen) and if clinical data is included the authorship order is different.

Publication: We are flexible with authorships. PhD thesis position for Anni, the leaders are Anniina and Heini. From Bergen Line and Cecilie (postdoc). Please note that the study authorship leads will need to be included (Åslaug Helland, Katriina Jalkanen) and if clinical data is included the authorship order is different.
